title: languas malaccensis
intro: Languas malaccensis, also known as the Malay ginger or Lesser galangal, is a type of plant belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian and Indian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Languas malaccensis include reducing fever, relieving nausea and digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Languas malaccensis is commonly used in infusions, decoctions, and as an ingredient in traditional remedies like soups and teas.
